content: Referral criteria and timing for palliative care. ASCO guidelines recommend early palliative care for all metastatic cancer patients. Triggers for referral: metastatic disease at diagnosis; high symptom burden (ESAS score ≥4 for any symptom); frequent emergency visits/hospitalizations; functional decline (ECOG ≥3, Karnofsky ≤50%); patient/family request; complex psychosocial needs; prognostic uncertainty; transition to hospice consideration. Assessment tools: ESAS (Edmonton Symptom Assessment System) - 9 symptoms rated 0-10; PHQ-9 for depression; GAD-7 for anxiety; PPS (Palliative Performance Scale) for functional status; SPICT (Supportive and Palliative Care Indicators Tool) identifies patients who may benefit. Hospice referral: life expectancy ≤6 months if disease follows usual course; declining performance status; stopping anticancer therapy (or switching to comfort-focused); recurrent hospitalizations; patient goals align with comfort care. Medicare hospice benefit covers two 90-day periods followed by unlimited 60-day periods if recertified.
